name: mini_corpus
description: A small embedded dataset for refract examples and testing. Contains 20 documents spanning Python programming, machine learning, and information retrieval.
documents: Sort a Python list using the sorted() built-in function. The sorted function returns a new list.
Neural networks learn hierarchical representations of data through backpropagation.
Retrieve relevant documents from a large corpus using inverted indices and ranking algorithms.
Use cosine similarity to measure vector closeness in high-dimensional embedding spaces.
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This sentence contains every letter of the alphabet.
Machine learning models require labeled training data and significant compute resources.
Python decorators are functions that modify the behavior of other functions at definition time.
Information retrieval systems rank documents by relevance using term frequency and inverse document frequency.
Deep learning architectures include Convolutional Neural Networks, Recurrent Neural Networks, and Transformers.
Binary search has O(log n) time complexity and requires a sorted input array.
Natural language processing enables computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language.
Vector databases store and index high-dimensional embeddings for fast approximate nearest neighbor search.
The PageRank algorithm assigns importance scores to web pages based on the link structure of the web.
Gradient descent optimizes model parameters by iteratively moving in the direction of steepest descent.
Tokenization splits text into individual tokens such as words, subwords, or characters.
Attention mechanisms allow neural networks to focus on the most relevant parts of an input sequence.
Python list comprehensions provide a concise way to create lists based on existing sequences.
Semantic search understands the meaning behind queries rather than matching exact keywords.
Cross-encoder rerankers score query-document pairs jointly for high-quality relevance estimation.
Embeddings map discrete objects like words or documents into continuous vector spaces.

what is the difference between semantic search and keyword search
